frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: noisy evidence integrates to a terminating bound governing speed, accuracy, and confidence

frameworks.name: decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: confidence in a prior decision carried forward to set the next decision's termination bound

frameworks.name: speed-accuracy trade-off
frameworks.one_line: strategic setting of the terminating bound balancing response time and correctness

core_question: How do subjects adjust strategy on a later decision given that the accuracy of an earlier decision is unknown?
key_assumptions: each decision is bounded evidence accumulation to a terminating threshold
subjects cannot directly access accuracy of past decisions
confidence serves as an internal proxy for past-decision accuracy
reward depends on both decisions being correct
manipulations: sequential pairing of two perceptual decisions under joint reward contingency
variation in first-decision confidence across trials
key_findings: speed and accuracy of individual decisions fit by noisy accumulation to a terminating bound
the second-decision termination bound was elevated in proportion to first-decision confidence
subjects adjusted speed-accuracy setting based on prior confidence
interpretations: confidence functions as a bridge linking sequential decisions
the brain rapidly and precisely modulates decision-termination mechanisms across stages
